lineage: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.

While Valens persecutes the Orthodox Christians in the East, a Usurper arises at Constantinople named Procopius: and at the Same Time an Earthquake and Inundation take Place and injure Several Cities.

ccel.title: NPNF2-02. Socrates and Sozomenus Ecclesiastical Histories

source.title: NPNF2-02. Socrates and Sozomenus Ecclesiastical Histories

title: While Valens persecutes the Orthodox Christians in the East, a Usurper arises at Constantinople named Procopius: and at the Same Time an Earthquake and Inundation take Place and injure Several Cities.

source.title: NPNF2-02. Socrates and Sozomenus Ecclesiastical Histories

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: While Valens was thus occupied in Syria, there arose a usurper at Constantinople named Procopius; who having collected a large body of troops in a very short time, meditated an expedition against the emperor.

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: This intelligence created extreme solicitude in the emperor’s mind and checked for a while the persecution he had commenced against all who dared to differ from him in opinion.

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: And while the commotions of a civil war were painfully anticipated, an earthquake occurred which did much damage to many cities.

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: The sea also changed its accustomed boundaries, and overflowed to such an extent in some places, that vessels might sail where roads had previously existed; and it retired so much from other places, that the ground became dry.

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: These events happened in the first consulate of the two emperors.
